revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23520214635|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:花園の御田舞]]


{{Image requesteds|Scenes from the event|cat=Itō District|date=May 2021}}
'''Hanazono no Onda Mai''' (花園の御田舞) is a form of traditional folk performing art passed down in Yanase, Hanazono, Katsuragi Town, Itō District, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan. It was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on January 21, 1981<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Hanazono no Onda Mai |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/199786 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]]}}</ref>.

The event begins early in the morning with a purification ritual at [[Shimo Hanazono Shrine]]. Participants then proceed to the Kōshō-ji Daishi Hall. In the afternoon, a series of dances are performed at the hall, mimicking agricultural tasks such as "circling the hoe," "plowing," "welcoming water," "calling the ox," "seedling beds," "rice planting," "harvesting," and "threshing"<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Hanazono no Onda Mai |url=https://www.jtco.or.jp/bunkakan/?act=detail&id=129&p=0&c=19 |website=www.jtco.or.jp |access-date=2023-09-28 |publisher=[[Japan Traditional Culture Promotion Organization]]}}</ref>. Although it was once conducted overnight over several days, it is now held biennially around the old lunar New Year<ref name=":1" />. Additionally, the venue has shifted from the former Hanazono Village to the Yanase district<ref name=":1" />.

This early spring ritual is a type of rice field play (田遊び), intended as a symbolic act of prayer for a year's harvest. It depicts the annual agricultural process through ancient songs and dances, preserving this traditional method of praying for abundant crops<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==
* {{Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database|302|111|Hanazono no Onda Mai}}
